segments.text: Range Rover just created their biggest branding disaster in 55 years. Social media users calling it cheap jewelry and belt buckles. This comes right after
Jaguar's rebrand disaster sent their sales crashing 97% from nearly 2,000 cars to just 49 cars sold in 1 month.
Now, Range Rover appears to be making the same mistake. But here is what most missed. This doubleR isn't replacing the classic Range Rover script on cars. It's
a smart symbol for electric future used on wheel caps. grills, merchandise, and digital spots where the full name won't fit in. Range Rover is walking a fine line between revolution and evolution.
Rather than scraping off decades of luxury branding, they're layering in a modern symbol design for today's digital first world. And crucially, they're preparing for the electric vehicle era.
Unlike Jaguar's rebrand, Range Rover kept their heritage while solving real problems. Want more amazing design stories like that? Subscribe.
